DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Douglas Park, Esq., (650) 814-3933, appearing for REZA R. DIBADJ, Plaintiff
Brennain J. Garber, Esq., (415) 278-9400, appearing for Lawrence K. Lo, Defendant

ARGUED. The Court stated that this matter was put over previously due to one of the parties being sick. Mr. Park presented his oral argument in support of his postion. Mr. Garber argued that any delay in this case is the fault of the plaintiff.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF REZA DIBADJ DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Affirmative defenses are sufficiently alleged and are not uncertain. Furthermore, based on the arguments advanced in the moving papers the omission of references to specific causes of action did not impede Plaintiff's ability to understand and analyze the defenses. The Court notes that parties filed severely oversized briefs without leave of Court, without table of authorities or table of contents. Therefore, in each brief all pages of Points and Authorities after page 10 are stricken. In the future such violations by the parties or their counsel will result in the entire briefs being stricken.Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not Reported. (D501)
